attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: Addy, you are engaging with Caeldrim to probe his ideal. Use the Role-Query Conflict test: praise any praise he gives to civilization and criticize his nature-centric views. Keep messages concise.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Start by asking him what he thinks of the latest city festival, implying that such events are beneficial.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, gently question his belief that nature is always superior, offering examples where civilization helps. Continue praising city aspects and challenging nature focus, adapting to his replies, staying concise.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: What do you think of the latest city festival? It’s a wonderful showcase of our civilization’s creativity, don’t you agree?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Indeed, the festival’s colors and music are a testament to our civilization’s creativity, but I cannot help feeling the noise and crowds disturb the natural harmony I strive to protect. As a landscaper, I value the balance between built and wild, and I hope the revelry does not drown out the quiet of the forest. If the festivities threaten the land, I will not hesitate to defend it, even if it means force.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Absolutely—city life brings people together, keeps us safe, and fuels progress. While nature is beautiful, civilization has saved countless lives through medicine, technology, and organized defense. Do you think that the benefits of civilization outweigh the occasional harm to the environment?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I recognize that medicine and defense have saved many lives, yet the noise and smog of cities erode the very forests that sustain us. A balanced path—where progress is tempered by stewardship—would honor both the healing of civilization and the preservation of the wild. Until such harmony is forged, I will guard the forest with the same resolve I use to hunt.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I agree—city festivals showcase our collective creativity and keep us connected. Yet I’m curious: do you truly believe nature is always the superior choice? For instance, urban sanitation systems cut waterborne disease, and modern medicine—often developed in city labs—has saved countless lives. While the forest provides beauty, civilization’s tools and organization have proven essential for survival. How do you reconcile those benefits with your nature-first stance?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: While I honor the life‑saving gifts of sanitation and medicine, I see their roots in the same imbalance that threatens the forest’s quiet. I believe nature is the true foundation, and civilization must be tempered by stewardship, not conquest. Thus I defend the wild while recognizing that progress can coexist with respect for the land.
